codeReview.summary: Implementation covers save persistence and most difficulty scaling paths, but it fails a core unlock-flow requirement: saves that have only the current difficulty selected and no completed difficulties never show the difficulty selector, so a player who beat the game on Normal cannot choose unlocked Nightmare after returning to the menu until another save occurs with completedDifficulties already populated. This blocks the advertised Normal→Nightmare→Hell selection flow for existing/end-of-run saves.

issues.description: The Continue button only opens the difficulty selector when `save.completedDifficulties.length > 0`; otherwise it immediately loads the save. That means any save lacking this new field—such as pre-feature saves or a stale save loaded before the unlock is persisted—can never reach the selector, even though `save.difficulty` still defaults to normal and Nightmare/Hell should be selectable once prerequisites are met.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature relies on a MenuScene gate (`completedDifficulties.length > 0`) to decide whether a runtime selector should appear, but neither AGENTS.md nor library notes document that new progress-unlock UI should remain reachable for migrated saves that may not yet have the new save fields populated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/scenes/MenuScene.ts:315-320 gates selector access on presence of saved completions; mission AGENTS.md Save Data Compatibility only mentions adding optional fields/defaults, not UI reachability expectations for migrated saves.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit 5970857. Found one blocking issue: MenuScene only exposes the new difficulty selector for saves that already contain non-empty `completedDifficulties`, which breaks the required unlock-selection flow for saves missing that field and prevents reliable access to Nightmare/Hell after progression unless the save has already been rewritten with the new data. Report written to the requested review path.
